title: ServiceClient = any erases type-checking for every Postgres call across all 10 Edge Functions
evidence: `_shared/mod.ts:167-168`: `// deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any\nexport type ServiceClient = any` — `getServiceClient()` returns this `any`. Every one of the 10 Edge Functions builds its DB access on this: `.from('rooms').select(...).eq(...).maybeSingle()` resolves to `any`, so `room`, `room.players`, `room.options`, `data`/`error` are all `any` at every call site (e.g. `submit_action/index.ts:130` `const players = (room.players ?? []) as StoredPlayer[]`, `join_room/index.ts:74` `const roomOptions = room.options as StoredOptions`). Because the base value is already `any`, the subsequent `as StoredPlayer[]`/`as StoredOptions` casts are asserting a shape `tsc --noEmit` cannot check against anything — a wrong column name (e.g. `.select('playerz')`), a JSONB shape drift, or a typo'd field would compile clean across all 10 functions and only fail at runtime in production, since `tsc --noEmit` strict is documented as the ONLY static gate for this Deno layer (no ESLint, no deno test wired into CI per tech-stack.md).
impact: Since Deno Edge Functions are the untrusted-input boundary (verify_jwt=false on all 10) and are not unit-tested in CI, a silent schema/column-name typo or a Supabase client version bump that changes `.select()`'s return shape has zero compile-time signal — it will only surface as a live 500/malformed-response in production against a real room. This is the single highest-leverage place strict TypeScript could catch a boundary bug, and it is opted out for all 10 functions at once via one shared `any`.
recommendation: Either (a) generate/vendor the Supabase Postgres schema types (`supabase gen types typescript`) and parametrize `createClient<Database>()` so `.from('rooms').select(...)` returns a real row shape instead of `any`, or (b) if generated types remain impractical in the Deno/esm.sh toolchain, keep `ServiceClient = any` at the client boundary but wrap every `.select(...)` result behind a single narrow parse/validate helper per table (mirroring the discipline already used for request bodies in `validate.ts`) so at least the read path gets the same runtime-checked treatment the write path already has.
acceptance_criteria: Either `getServiceClient()` returns a typed client (parametrized with a `Database`/schema type) so `room.players`/`room.options` etc. are structurally checked by `tsc --noEmit`, or every `.select()` result flowing into a `StoredPlayer[]`/`StoredOptions` cast passes through a runtime shape-check helper before the cast is applied.
No net-new `as <Type>` cast is applied directly to a raw `any`-typed Supabase query result without an intervening runtime check.
